chapters.title: Resistance Appears Wherever Important Work Begins

summaryBullets: Treating resistance as a predictable force that appears before meaningful work creates clearer judgment.
Creative struggle becomes easier to manage when resistance is expected instead of personalized.
This matters because important work attracts avoidance because it changes who you become.
A common mistake is treating avoidance as proof you are unsuited for the work.
The pattern becomes easier to see when the closer the work gets to what matters, the stronger the distraction becomes.
A practical starting move is to name the resistance when it appears, start before you feel ready, and separate the work from the mood.
keyTakeaways: Treating resistance as a predictable force that appears before meaningful work creates clearer judgment. This perspective helps people make clearer decisions and follow through with more discipline when stakes rise.
practice: name the resistance when it appears
start before you feel ready
separate the work from the mood
show up anyway

chapters.title: Professionals Show Up Differently Than Amateurs

summaryBullets: Working like a professional who shows up consistently instead of waiting for inspiration creates clearer judgment.
Discipline beats mood when the goal is finished work.
This matters because creative momentum builds when the standard is to appear and work.
A common mistake is treating inspiration as a requirement before beginning.
The pattern becomes easier to see when days keep disappearing because the feeling never seems perfect enough.
A practical starting move is to keep a start time, define the work block clearly, and show up even on low energy days.
keyTakeaways: Working like a professional who shows up consistently instead of waiting for inspiration creates clearer judgment. This perspective helps people make clearer decisions and follow through with more discipline when stakes rise.
practice: keep a start time
define the work block clearly
show up even on low energy days
judge the day by whether you worked
